\noindent Next, we sample $n$ points from a normal
distribution, to generate $y$ values for each $x$
value and fit the corresponding regression line.
We repeat this process \ttblue{rep} times.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
_line_frames = []
for i in range(1, int(rep) + 1):
    epsilon = np.random.normal(loc=0.0, scale=sigma, 
size=n)
    y_values = beta_0 + beta_1 * x_values + epsilon
    slope_i, intercept_i = np.polyfit(x_values, y_values, 
deg=1)
    predicted_values = intercept_i + slope_i * x_extended
    _line_frames.append(pd.DataFrame({'x': x_extended, 'y':
 predicted_values, 'rep': i}))
simulated_lines = pd.concat(_line_frames, 
ignore_index=True)
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
